instruction: Rewrite the content to meet insurance regulatory and compliance standards.

input.draft_text:
Importance & Methods of ULIP Renewal
What is ULIP?
A ULIP is a market-linked life insurance policy wherein the invested premiums are directed to specific funds (that the policyholder chooses). Each fund contains a diversified portfolio of securities that are traded on the financial market. As the market performs, the value of the portfolio changes and so does your investment.
Being life insurance plans, ULIPs have insurance coverage as well. The life insured is covered during the policy tenure. If the insured passes away before maturity, a death benefit is paid. Alternatively, if the insured survives the chosen policy tenure, the maturity benefit is paid.
Overview of ULIP renewal
ULIPs are medium to long-term insurance plans wherein the tenure usually starts at 5 years and goes up to the specified maximum tenure. Some ULIPs even run till 100 or 99 years of age allowing lifelong protection.
The premiums for ULIPs can be paid in three ways –
Single premium – The premium is payable once when you buy the ULIP.
Limited premium – The premium is paid for a limited number of years which is lower than the total policy tenure. For instance, in a 15-year ULIP policy, a limited premium might be needed for 10 years only. You pay for 10 years but enjoy coverage for 15 years.
Regular premium – wherein you pay the premium throughout the chosen policy tenure. So, if you choose a policy tenure of 15 years, you have to pay the premium for 15 years.
Under limited and regular premium policies, you have to pay the premiums regularly, for the stipulated tenure. After the payment of the first premium, the payment of the subsequent premium is called ULIP renewal. As you pay the premium on the due date, the coverage is renewed and it runs for the duration for which the premium has been duly paid.
Importance/ significance of renewing your ULIP policy
Renewing your ULIP policy is important for the following reasons –
Continued coverage
As you renew the ULIP regularly, you can enjoy complete insurance protection allowed by the policy. In the case of uncertainty, the ULIP will pay the death benefit to provide financial security to your family.
Better returns
As you keep paying the premiums, your fund value keeps on increasing. This increases your investments and you can earn higher returns on the enhanced investments. Moreover, the compounded returns are calculated on the increased corpus allowing better returns.
Avoid lapse and reduced benefits
If the ULIP is not renewed on time, it lapses. A lapsed policy has various implications. Have a look –
Lapse in the first 5 years – if you fail to renew the plan within the lock-in period of 5 years, your fund value would be transferred to the discontinued policy fund. The money would stay invested in the fund till the lock-in period is completed. The returns would be affected as the discontinued policy fund offers very low returns. The charges would be deducted from the fund value which would further reduce the value of your investments. After the lock-in period is over, the policy would be deemed to be surrendered. You would receive the available fund value and the coverage would be terminated1.
Lapse after the first 5 years – if the lock-in period is over and you discontinue premium payments, your policy would be converted to a paid-up policy. The fund would remain invested and you would continue earning returns on the same. The applicable charges would be deducted. You can choose to continue the paid-up policy till maturity and receive the available fund value or surrender the policy before maturity and get the surrender value1.
Reduction in death benefit – ULIPs usually pay higher of the sum assured or the available fund value if the insured dies. While the above-mentioned points outline what happens to the fund value, the sum assured is also reduced. Reduction in the sum assured is done in proportion to the number of premiums paid v/s the number of premiums payable. This is called the paid-up sum assured and it is used when assessing the death benefit1.
For instance, say you bought a 20-year ULIP and renewed it for 10 years. The sum assured was ₹10 lakhs. If you do not renew the coverage, the paid-up sum assured would be calculated as – 10 years/20 years X ₹10 lakhs = ₹5 lakhs
By renewing on time, you can avoid these circumstances and enjoy full benefits.
Tax benefits on premium
ULIP premiums are allowed as a deduction up to ₹1.5 lakhs2under Section 80C of the Income Tax Act 1961, under old regime of Tax if all the conditions of section 80C are satisfied.
Different ways to renew your ULIP
Renewal of a ULIP can be done using two primary modes – the online mode and the offline mode. Each mode offers various alternatives for premium payment to renew the coverage. Have a look –
Online mode of renewal
Premium payment can be done through the following means -
Credit card
Debit card
Mobile wallets
UPI
NEFT
RTGS
IMPS
Direct debit from your bank account
Online fund transfers, etc.
Offline mode of renewal
Premium can be paid through the following means –
Cash
Cheque
Demand draft
Things to keep in mind while renewing your ULIP online
When renewing your ULIP using the online premium payment mode, here are a few things to keep in mind –
Ensure your bank allows the net banking facility for online fund transfers through your account. Also, register for the facility for seamless payments.
If you are using UPI, link your bank account to your UPI ID and maintain optimal funds in the account for a successful transaction
In the case of mobile wallets, ensure that the wallet has sufficient balance to pay the premium
Credit and debit cards should be used carefully with the details entered correctly
Try and pay using the insurer’s website since it is secured and encrypted
Avoid using public Wi-Fi for payment as it is prone to cyberattacks
Keep your password/PIN/OTP confidential. Do not share it with anyone, even a company representative
Use a fast internet connection to allow the transaction to go through without any lags or failures
Beware of cyberattacks and crimes and take preventive measures to avoid them like installing antimalware in your system, etc.
Things to keep in mind while renewing your ULIP offline
If you are renewing the ULIP offline, here are some things to remember –
If you are paying in cash, remember that the maximum premium that would be accepted in cash would be ₹50,0003. If the premium is higher, choose to pay via a cheque or demand draft
If paying through a cheque, maintain sufficient funds in your bank account to avoid cheque bounce
Mention the correct policy number on the cheque or demand draft
Draw the cheque or draft in favour of the insurance company mentioning the correct name
Conclusion
ULIPs run for longer tenures and regular renewals become important to enjoy the coverage that the policy offers. Moreover, regular renewals help in enhancing the invested corpus so that the compounded returns are calculated on a higher amount. This helps you get good returns if the market is performing well and grow an optimal fund for your needs. So, renew your ULIPs on time by paying the premiums online or offline. If you miss the due date, use the grace period and pay the premium to enjoy complete coverage.
FAQs
How can I renew my ULIP policy?
You can renew your ULIP policy by paying the due premium within the due date or within the allowed grace period.
Should I renew my ULIP policy?
It is recommended to renew your ULIP policy to enjoy complete insurance coverage, corpus creation through compounded returns and tax benefits. Moreover, with regular renewals, you can build a suitable corpus for your financial goals.
Why is ULIP renewal important?
ULIP renewal is important because of the various benefits that it provides. These benefits include tax savings under Section 80C, full insurance protection, better returns through enhanced investments and more.
Reference
https://www.bajajallianzlife.com/content/dam/balic/pdf/ulip/invest-protect-goal-sl.pdf
https://incometaxindia.gov.in/tutorials/20.%20tax%20benefits%20due%20to%20health%20insurance.pdf
https://taxguru.in/corporate-law/irda-insurance-premiume-payment-cash-rs-50000-transaction-subject-customer-quoting-pan.html#:~:text=Acceptance%20Stage%3A&text=a)%20With%20a%20view%20to,to%20the%20customer%20quoting%20PAN.
Other references for information gathering
https://economictimes.indiatimes.com/wealth/personal-finance-news/ulip-renewal-tide-against-trend-premium-collection-jumps-44/articleshow/4984689.cms?from=mdr
https://caknowledge.com/ulip-renewal/
https://www.financialexpress.com/money/renewal-of-existing-ulip-remains-a-sound-financial-decision-find-out/2243158/

IN THIS POLICY, THE INVESTMENT RISK IN INVESTMENT PORTFOLIO IS BORNE BY THE POLICYHOLDER.
The Unit Linked Insurance Products do not offer any liquidity during the first five years of the contract. The policyholder will not be able to surrender or withdraw the monies invested in Unit Linked Insurance Products completely or partially till the end of the fifth year.
ULIPs are different from the traditional insurance products and are subject to the risk factors. The premium paid in ULIPs are subject to investment risks associated with capital markets and the NAVs of the units may go up or down based on the performance of fund and factors influencing the capital market and the insured is responsible for his/her decisions. Bajaj Allianz Life Insurance Company Limited is only the name of the Life Insurance Company and does not in any way indicate the quality of the contract, its future prospects or returns. Please know the associated risks and the applicable charges, from your Insurance agent or the Intermediary or policy document issued by the insurance company. The various funds offered under this contract are the names of the funds and do not in any way indicate the quality of these plans, their future prospects and returns.
The views stated in this article are not to be construed as investment advice and readers are suggested to seek independent financial advice before making any investment decisions. For more details on risk factors, terms and conditions please read the sales brochure & policy document (available on www.bajajallianzlife.com) carefully before concluding a sale. No.: 116, CIN: U66010PN2001PLC015959, Call us on toll free No.: 1800 209 7272, Mail us: customercare@bajajallianz.co.in
Tax benefits as per prevailing Section 80C of the Income Tax Act shall apply. You are requested to consult your tax consultant and obtain independent advice for eligibility before claiming any benefit under the policy.
